module_a.title: Module A — Internal Control
module_a.description: Self-assessment path. The manufacturer prepares the technical documentation, declares conformity, and affixes the CE marking. No notified body is involved.

module_b_c.title: Module B+C — EU Type Examination
module_b_c.description: A notified body examines a representative example of the product (Module B), then the manufacturer declares conformity to that type (Module C).

module_h.title: Module H — Full Quality Assurance
module_h.description: A notified body audits and approves a full quality system covering design, manufacturing, and final inspection. Surveillance audits are recurring.

european_certification.title: European Cybersecurity Certification
european_certification.description: Conformity is demonstrated through a product's certificate issued under an EU cybersecurity certification scheme (CSA).

notifiedBody.title: Notified body
notifiedBody.description: Record the notified body that is performing the conformity assessment. The ID and scope go directly onto the Declaration of Conformity.
notifiedBody.name: Name

name.title: Notified body name
name.body: Full legal name of the notified body (e.g. "TÜV Rheinland LGA Products GmbH", "BSI Assurance UK Ltd"). Must match the name published in the NANDO database — case and punctuation sensitive.
name.ref: CRA Annex V(4) — when a notified body is involved (Module B+C or Module H), the Declaration of Conformity must state its name alongside the four-digit identification number.

id.title: Notified body number
id.body: The four-digit identification number the European Commission assigned to the notified body (e.g. 0197 for TÜV Rheinland). Find yours by looking the body up in the NANDO database: ec.europa.eu/growth/tools-databases/nando.
id.ref: CRA Annex V(4) — the notified body number is part of the CE marking on products requiring third-party assessment and must be printed on the Declaration of Conformity.

scope.title: Scope of the notified body's activity
scope.body: One sentence describing what the notified body did — e.g. "EU-type examination of the product as per Module B" or "Quality system audit under Module H". Taken verbatim from the notified body's certificate.
scope.ref: CRA Annex V(4) — the declaration must state the specific conformity-assessment activity performed, not just the body's name. Customs and market surveillance authorities use this to verify you used the right module.

steps.title: Workflow
steps.notesPlaceholder: Add internal notes, links to evidence, or audit reference.

checklist_complete.title: CRA checklist at 100%
checklist_complete.description: All applicable Annex I items on the checklist are marked Completed.

sbom_active.title: Active SBOM scanned
sbom_active.description: The active SBOM has been scanned and the CVE results are reviewed.

technical_documentation_prepared.title: Technical documentation prepared
technical_documentation_prepared.description: Annex II technical file is compiled: description, design, risk, testing, DoC.

risk_assessment_complete.title: Risk assessment completed
risk_assessment_complete.description: Product's cybersecurity risk has been assessed and documented.

notified_body_selected.title: Notified body selected
notified_body_selected.description: A notified body with the required scope has been engaged.

eu_type_examination_passed.title: EU type examination passed
eu_type_examination_passed.description: Notified body has examined a representative sample and issued the certificate.

conformity_to_type_verified.title: Conformity to type verified
conformity_to_type_verified.description: Production is verified to match the examined type.

quality_system_documented.title: Quality system documented
quality_system_documented.description: Design, manufacturing, and final-inspection procedures are documented.

quality_system_approved.title: Quality system approved
quality_system_approved.description: Notified body has approved the quality system.

surveillance_audit_scheduled.title: Surveillance audit scheduled
surveillance_audit_scheduled.description: Recurring surveillance audit planned with the notified body.

eu_certification_obtained.title: EU certificate obtained
eu_certification_obtained.description: Product holds a valid certificate under an EU cybersecurity certification scheme.

declaration_prepared.title: Declaration prepared
declaration_prepared.description: DoC text, manufacturer details, and referenced standards are ready.

ce_marking_affixed.title: CE marking affixed
ce_marking_affixed.description: The CE marking (with notified body number where applicable) is affixed to the product.

doc.title: Issue the Declaration of Conformity
doc.description: Generate the EU Declaration of Conformity, auto-filled from the product, organization, and notified body details above.
doc.stepsIncomplete: All workflow steps must be complete or N/A before the declaration can be issued.
doc.profileIncomplete: Your company profile is missing required fields. Open Settings → Organization to finish it.

toast.commentRequired: Add a note before changing the status — every status change is recorded in the audit log.
toast.fileTooLarge: File is over the 2 MB upload limit.
toast.unsupportedMime: That file type isn't supported. Images, PDFs, Office documents, and plain text are accepted.
toast.uploadFailed: Couldn't upload the file. Please try again.

conversation.empty: No comments yet. Start the thread with the first note.
conversation.placeholder: Add a note explaining what changed and why…
conversation.noteRequired: Comments are saved to the audit log and can't be edited or deleted.
conversation.pendingChange: Pending change: {from} → {to}. Hit Save to commit.

standards.title: Harmonised standards & presumption of conformity
standards.intro: A product conforming to a harmonised standard cited in the Official Journal is presumed to conform to the Annex I essential requirements it covers (Article 27). Common specifications and European cybersecurity certification schemes can confer the same presumption.
standards.statusNote: The dedicated CRA standards are still being developed under standardisation request M/606 (accepted by CEN, CENELEC and ETSI in April 2025) and are not yet cited in the Official Journal.

part.part_i: Annex I Part I — security requirements
part.part_ii: Annex I Part II — vulnerability handling

en_18031_1.title: Internet-connected radio equipment — network protection
en_18031_1.note: Harmonised under the Radio Equipment Directive; a practical baseline for Annex I Part I until the dedicated CRA standards are cited.

en_18031_2.title: Radio equipment — protection of personal data and privacy
en_18031_2.note: Harmonised under the Radio Equipment Directive; addresses confidentiality and data-minimisation expectations.

en_18031_3.title: Radio equipment — protection against fraud
en_18031_3.note: Harmonised under the Radio Equipment Directive; addresses data-integrity expectations.

cra_horizontal_product.title: CRA horizontal standard — product security requirements
cra_horizontal_product.note: Being developed under standardisation request M/606; not yet cited in the Official Journal.

cra_horizontal_vuln_handling.title: CRA horizontal standard — vulnerability handling
cra_horizontal_vuln_handling.note: Being developed under standardisation request M/606; targeted ahead of the other horizontal standards.

common_specifications.title: Commission common specifications
common_specifications.note: The Commission may adopt these by implementing act where harmonised standards are absent or insufficient.

eucc_scheme.title: EUCC — Common Criteria certification scheme
eucc_scheme.note: A certificate at the relevant assurance level can confer a presumption of conformity for the corresponding requirements.
